page: UpCloud HIMEM-6xCPU-128GB is a memory-optimized virtual server running on the KVM hypervisor. It features 6 vCPUs mapped to 6 physical cores of an AMD EPYC 7542 processor clocked at 2.0 GHz, utilizing a shared CPU allocation model. The server is equipped with 128.0 GB of RAM, providing a high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 21.33 GB per core. Local storage is provided via a bundled 300 GB SSD, and the instance includes one complimentary public IPv4 address.

Performance benchmarks indicate a clear tradeoff between raw compute power and memory capacity. Single-core and multi-core CPU stress tests register in the bottom 10% and 25% respectively, reflecting the shared CPU allocation and modest 2.0 GHz clock speed. However, memory-intensive operations perform more competitively. Small-block cached memory read bandwidth and single-threaded compression tasks achieve average tier results. Large-block uncached memory read and write bandwidths are weaker, placing in the bottom 25%, while PassMark memory scores also fall into the lower quartile.

Database and application benchmarks show balanced capabilities. Redis SET operations reach an average of 8.6 million operations per second, and PassMark database operations align with mid-tier performance. For specialized workloads, the server handles small-model LLM inference at average speeds, though larger models or high-throughput static web serving expose performance bottlenecks. This hardware profile offers qualitative cost efficiency for memory-bound workloads that do not require sustained, high-performance compute cores, making it suitable for caching layers, relational databases, and memory-resident data processing.
description: UpCloud HIMEM-6xCPU-128GB is a memory-optimized KVM virtual server configured with 6 AMD EPYC 7542 vCPUs running at 2.0 GHz and 128.0 GB of RAM, yielding a high ratio of 21.33 GB of memory per core. It includes a bundled 300 GB local SSD and one public IPv4 address. Benchmark data reveals a performance profile tailored for memory-heavy tasks rather than raw compute power; while CPU stress tests rank in the lower quartiles due to shared CPU allocation, memory-centric benchmarks like small-block cached reads and Redis SET operations achieve average tier results. This server is qualitatively cost-efficient for workloads requiring large memory footprints without intensive CPU demands, such as caching systems, in-memory databases, and moderate data processing tasks.
og_description: UpCloud HIMEM-6xCPU-128GB is a memory-optimized KVM virtual server configured with 6 AMD EPYC vCPUs, 128 GB of RAM, and a 300 GB local SSD, designed for memory-intensive database workloads.

tagline: A memory-optimized virtual server featuring a high memory-to-core ratio and bundled SSD storage for memory-intensive workloads.

128.0 GB RAM providing 21.33 GB per core
Average performance in Redis and database benchmarks
Suited for in-memory databases, caching, and data processing
